display.purpose: The race countdown number only. The single biggest moment on the surface.

headline.purpose: Hero active-session duration and the large sport badge.

title.purpose: Page and section titles; card titles step down to ~0.83rem/700.

body.purpose: Inputs, descriptions, schedule labels. 14px base, 65-75ch for prose.

label.purpose: Uppercase eyebrows, day-of-week headers, sport badges.

shadows.name: resting-card
shadows.purpose: Default barely-there lift for cards and panels off the canvas.

shadows.name: lifted
shadows.purpose: Elements that rise above the page: popovers, expanded activity detail. Use rarely.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions and progress fills.

motion.name: duration-fast
motion.purpose: Hover, focus, and color state changes.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single committed action on a surface (Connect with Strava, Save and apply).

components.name: Add Button (dashed)
components.description: Adds an item to a list (+ Add Phase). Dashed border is the 'add' signal.

components.name: Start-Flow CTA (solid)
components.description: Starts a flow (Load preset, Start AI questionnaire). Solid border distinguishes it from the dashed 'add' button.

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Labeled form field used throughout the Plan Builder and AI questionnaire.

components.name: Card
components.description: Top-level surface for a section of the dashboard or builder.
components.html:
Race Info

Pick a ready-made plan, then review and tweak before saving.

components.name: Nav Bar
components.description: Sticky top bar with links and the ever-present race countdown.
components.html: Edit Plan« Race in 112 days

components.name: Sport Badge
components.description: Uppercase discipline label paired with sport color so the schedule reads in grayscale.
components.html: RUN SWIM BIKE

narrative.overview: This is the interface of a coach who is steady at every Tuesday session and genuinely fired up on the start line. For the long middle of a training block it stays calm and out of the way: a light, uncluttered surface that answers 'what do I do today?' first, then lets the athlete close the tab and go train. The intensity is held in reserve, rising only for the moments that earn it: race week, race day, a milestone. Underneath the calm is real substance; the plan reacts to actual Strava performance, so the numbers are honest and never dressed up as vanity progress. One code path carries a 5K runner and a full Ironman alike.
keyCharacteristics: Light theme, near-white surfaces on a soft gray canvas, generous breathing room.
A single committed accent (Strava orange) that means status and action, never decoration.
Sport-coded but never color-only: every colored dot is backed by a text badge.
Restrained motion: state feedback, not choreography.
Day-first hierarchy: today's session and progress to race day lead every screen.

rules.name: The Two-Orange Rule
rules.body: Bright #FC4C02 is for large display and decoration only (text 24px+ bold, dots, fills, rings). Anything smaller or interactive uses #CC4011. Bright orange as small text on white is a contrast bug.

rules.name: The Color-Plus-Label Rule
rules.body: Sport color never carries meaning alone. Every colored dot or fill is paired with a text badge (SWIM, RUN, BIKE, BRICK, REST). The schedule must read correctly in grayscale.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Inter (or the system stack) for everything. No display or serif face. Hierarchy is earned through weight and a wide scale jump, not a second font.

rules.name: The Hairline-First Rule
rules.body: Reach for a 1px or 1.5px hairline border before a shadow. Structure is drawn with strokes; shadow is only an ambient lift on true surfaces.

dos: Do use Action Orange (#CC4011) for any orange text under large size and for all filled buttons. It clears AA at 4.87:1; bright #FC4C02 does not (3.4:1).
Do reserve bright Strava Orange (#FC4C02) for large display (the countdown), sport dots, fills, and the today/race-day ring.
Do pair every sport color with a text badge so the schedule reads in grayscale.
Do lead each screen with today's session and progress to race day; reach deeper data on purpose.
Do draw structure with hairline borders first, shadow only as an ambient lift.
Do keep one font family (Inter / system) and build hierarchy from weight and scale.
Do hold visible keyboard focus on every interactive element and honor prefers-reduced-motion.
donts: Don't build a generic SaaS dashboard: no identical KPI card grids, no big-number-plus-label hero-metric template, no purple gradients.
Don't gamify: no badges, confetti, streak guilt, or aggressive nudges. Motivation is the real race, not points.
Don't overload with data: no wall of competing charts, no showing every metric at once.
Don't use background-clip:text gradient text, or any gradient as a decorative accent.
Don't use a colored border-left or border-right stripe as an accent; use a full border, a background tint, or a leading badge.
Don't spend the bright orange on decoration that is not a real status or milestone; its rarity is what gives race day its weight.
Don't animate layout properties or add choreographed page-load sequences; motion is state feedback only.
